factors.summary: The global logistics services industry is highly competitive, with many competitors having significantly more resources and actively pursuing new technologies.

evidence.text: The global logistics services industry is intensely competitive and is expected to remain so for the foreseeable future. There are a large number of companies competing in one or more segments of the industry, but the number of firms with a global network that offer a full complement of logistics services is more limited. Nevertheless, many of these competitors have significantly more resources than Expeditors, and are actively pursuing acquisition opportunities and are developing new technologies to gain competitive advantages.
evidence.subsection: Our industry is highly competitive, and failure to compete or respond to customer requirements could damage our business and results of operations.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: The industry is highly competitive, with major competitors having more resources and actively pursuing new technologies, posing a significant threat.

factors.summary: The company faces challenges in retaining employees due to the transition back to in-office work post-pandemic.

evidence.text: As the pandemic restrictions ease, we are requiring employees to return to the office. As a result, for those individuals that prefer working remotely, we may experience a higher degree of turnover and lower employee satisfaction in the near future.
evidence.subsection: We are dependent on our personnel and any inability to hire, develop or retain our employees may have a negative impact on our operations.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company may face increased employee turnover due to the transition back to in-office work post-pandemic.

factors.summary: The company is facing headwinds due to increased competition and economic uncertainties from the global pandemic affecting international trade.

evidence.text: Everything that affects international trade has the potential to expand or contract our primary market and adversely impact our operating results. For example, international trade is influenced by...changes in the price and readily available quantities of oil and other petroleum-related products.
evidence.subsection: Any reduction in international commerce or disruption in global trade may adversely impact our business and operating results.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Economic uncertainties from the global pandemic and increased competition are negatively impacting the company's operating results.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing challenges in meeting customer demand due to insufficient transportation capacity post-pandemic.

evidence.text: When market demand significantly exceeds available capacity in a given market, which has been increasingly the case for various services and markets since the beginning of the pandemic in 2020, we may not always be able to find acceptable transportation solutions to meet our customers’ needs or the routing and delivery of freight may be subject to delays that are outside of our control.
evidence.subsection: We are dependent on our personnel and any inability to hire, develop or retain our employees may have a negative impact on our operations.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company struggles to meet customer demand due to insufficient transportation capacity post-pandemic, indicating a negative trend.

factors.summary: The company is facing pressure to accept expanded contractual terms, which could negatively impact pricing power.

evidence.text: Increased competition and competitors' acceptance of expanded contractual terms coupled with customers’ dissatisfaction with elevated rates, scarce capacity and extended transit times could result in loss of business, reduced revenues, reduced margins, higher operating costs or loss of market share.
evidence.subsection: Our industry is highly competitive, and failure to compete or respond to customer requirements could damage our business and results of operations.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Pressure from competitors accepting expanded terms and customer dissatisfaction could reduce the company's pricing power.
